## [00:00 - 01:32]

This is the pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer and today we're going to talk about why Meta's review process is so slow. Let's go. Why does it have to be this way? These two ad sets are part of a leads campaign that are both promoting a bot beta test. The first ad set was approved a couple days ago. No issues. The second ad set is a duplication of the first. The only change was to the location. I didn't touch the ad. I didn't touch the optimization. Same placement. Same everything else. Yet that second ad set sits under review several hours later. Why isn't this review instantaneous? We know that everything but the location was approved with the first ad set. How could the change in location possibly impact whether or not it's approved? Yes, there are specific laws and restrictions by location. But that's often due to things like alcohol and gambling and things like that. Spoiler, there is nothing about this ad that would be problematic based on location. But I'm stuck waiting in this queue just because that's the way it's always been. You'd think that improving this inefficiency would put more money in Meta's pockets. Some of these reviews should be immediate. What do you think? So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
